zh.lifeSummary: 當Gilbert de Notton - Constable of Chester於大約1154年出生在英國越可爾諾頓時，他的父親Reginald De Notton18歲，母親Nest FitzLeofwin20歲。他於大約1189年與Juliana de Wigan結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1222年在英國藍卡爾享年69歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gilbert de Notton - Constable of Chester于大约1154年出生在英国越可爾諾頓时，他的父亲Reginald De Notton18岁，母亲Nest FitzLeofwin20岁。他于大约1189年与Juliana de Wigan结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1222年在英国藍卡爾享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Notton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Notton in Lacock (Wilts) or Notton in Maiden Newton (Dorset), both of which are recorded as Natton and Netton in the 13th century. The place-names derive from Old English nēat ‘cattle’ + tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’. There has probably been some confusion with Noton .

variants.name: Natton
meanings: English: variant of both   Notton   and   Noton  .

variants.name: Noton
meanings: English: locative name from Notton (WR Yorks), which is recorded as Notone in 1086 and Nocton in 1230. The place-name derives from Old English hnoc ‘wether sheep’ + tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’. There has probably been some confusion with Notton , and perhaps also with Knutton and Nocton .

nameMeanings.name: Constable
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southeastern): occupational name for the law-enforcement officer of a parish, from Middle English, Old French conestable, cunestable, from Late Latin comes stabuli ‘officer of the stable’. The title was also borne by various other officials during the Middle Ages, including the chief officer of a household or court, or a military officer, and this may in some cases be the source of the surname.
Americanized form of Dutch Constapel, an occupational name for the chief gunner aboard a ship or in the garrison of a fort.

variants.name: Costabile
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Aldo, Antonio, Ciro, Nino, Agnese, Angelo, Duilio, Enrico, Ercole, Franco, Palma.
Italian (southern):
occupational name from medieval Greek kontostablēs ‘chamberlain’, from medieval Latin comes stabuli, literally ‘count of the stable’ (see Constable ).

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Chester
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Chester, the county seat of Cheshire, or from any of various smaller places called with this word (as for example Little Chester in Derbyshire, Chester le Street in County Durham, or Chesters in Northumberland), which is from Old English ceaster ‘Roman fort or walled city’ (from Latin castra ‘legionary camp’).

variants.name: Cheshire
meanings: English: habitational name for someone from the county of Cheshire in northwestern England, the name of which is early recorded as Cestrescire, from the name of the county seat, Chester , + Old English scīr ‘district, division’.

variants.name: Chesterman
meanings: English: possibly a topographic name for someone who lived by a Roman fort, Old English ceaster, or a habitational name for someone from any of the places mentioned at Chester .

nameMeanings.name: Gilbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gisil ‘pledge’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. This was the name of the founder of the only native British religious order (abolished at the Dissolution of the Monasteries), St Gilbert of Sempringham ( ?1083–1189 ), in whose honour it is still sometimes bestowed, especially among Roman Catholics. It gained a wider currency in the 19th century.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: When Gilbert de Notton - Constable of Chester was born about 1154, in Notton, Yorkshire, England, his father, Reginald De Notton, was 18 and his mother, Nest FitzLeofwin, was 20. He married Juliana de Wigan about 1189. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1222, in Lancashire, England, at the age of 69.

Il a épousé Juliana de Wigan environ 1189. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1222, à Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 69 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Gilbert de Notton - Constable of Chester(이)가 대략 1154년 영국 요르크실르 노턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Reginald De Notton(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Nest FitzLeofwin(은)는 20세였다. 그는 대략 1189년 Juliana de Wigan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1222년 영국 랭카실르에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Juliana de Wigan около 1189 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1222 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 69 лет.
